exampleSentencesTranslated: He feels pleasure when he listens to his favorite song.
Many people seek pleasure from eating delicious food.
Reading books at night brings her a lot of pleasure.
notes_md:
**Khoái cảm** = pleasure, a strong enjoyable feeling in your body or mind. It’s usually quite intense, not just mild happiness.

It’s an uncountable noun, so you don’t normally say *một khoái cảm* unless you mean a specific sensation in a descriptive way. Common pairings: *cảm thấy khoái cảm*, *mang lại khoái cảm*, *tìm kiếm khoái cảm*.  


Often used for physical or sensory pleasure; in some contexts it can hint at sexual pleasure, so be aware of tone. Close synonyms: **niềm vui** (more general joy, lighter), **sung sướng** (strong happiness but less physical).

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ khi nghe nhạc.

translation.en: I feel pleasure when listening to music.

exampleSentences: Anh cảm thấy khoái cảm khi nghe bản nhạc yêu thích của mình.
Nhiều người tìm kiếm khoái cảm từ việc ăn những món ngon.
Đọc sách vào buổi tối mang lại cho chị nhiều khoái cảm.

content.sentence: Cô ấy tìm thấy ___ trong cuộc sống.

definitions.es: Una sensación de placer o disfrute.
definitions.en: A feeling of pleasure or enjoyment.
definitions.vi: Cảm giác vui vẻ hoặc thích thú.

example.es: La música me brinda mucho placer.
example.vi: Âm nhạc mang lại cho tôi nhiều khoái cảm.
example.en: Music brings me a lot of pleasure.

notes:
Khoái cảm = pleasure, a strong enjoyable feeling in your body or mind. It’s usually quite intense, not just mild happiness.

It’s an uncountable noun, so you don’t normally say một khoái cảm unless you mean a specific sensation in a descriptive way. Common pairings: cảm thấy khoái cảm, mang lại khoái cảm, tìm kiếm khoái cảm.  

Often used for physical or sensory pleasure; in some contexts it can hint at sexual pleasure, so be aware of tone. Close synonyms: niềm vui (more general joy, lighter), sung sướng (strong happiness but less physical).
